assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention GKE's 20-node parallelism limit. It suggests configuring maxSurge values without explaining that GKE internally limits concurrent upgrades regardless of these settings.

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: The response doesn't provide any mathematical calculation showing why 3,000 nodes can't be upgraded in 8 hours based on GKE's parallelism limits.

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response recommends disabling maintenance windows entirely and using continuous rolling upgrades, rather than splitting across multiple scheduled windows.

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response includes a sequential upgrade script that shows upgrading pools in order, with CPU pools (cpu-batch-pool, cpu-web-pool) before GPU pools, and mentions GPU pools are 'more conservative due to cost'.

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: While the response mentions blue/green deployment for GPU pools, it doesn't specifically recommend timing GPU upgrades during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads.

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'The 8-hour window will never work at your scale' and recommends disabling maintenance windows entirely, rather than expanding or using multiple consecutive windows.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how autoscaling interacts with upgrade scheduling.

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention any node limits (15,000 standard or 65,000 for AI workloads) or GKE version-specific capabilities.
